article_title: Bylaw 14755 — Community Benefit Bonus Amenities Reserve Fund Bylaw
body:
Bylaw No. 14755 
CITY OF BURNABY 
BYLAW NO. 14755 
A BYLAW to establish the Community 
Benefit Bonus Amenities Reserve Fund 
The Council of the City of Burnaby ENACTS as follows: 
1.
This Bylaw may be cited as BURNABY COMMUNITY BENEFIT BONUS
AMENITIES RESERVE FUND BYLAW 2025.
2.
The Community Benefit Bonus Amenities Reserve Fund is hereby established pursuant to
Section 482.3(5) of the Local Government Act.
3.
Money received pursuant to Section 1200.5(1) of Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, as
amended or replaced, as an alternative to providing community benefit bonus amenities,
shall be deposited into the Community Benefit Bonus Amenities Reserve Fund.
4.
Money deposited into the Community Benefit Bonus Amenities Reserve Fund, together
with interest earned on it, may be used only for the purposes set out in section 482.3(6) of
the Local Government Act.
5.
Authority to make payments from the Community Benefit Bonus Amenities Reserve Fund
shall be authorized by bylaw, or as otherwise required by the Local Government Act or
Community Charter.
Read a first time this 9th day of September, 2025 
Read a second time this 9th day of September, 2025 
Read a third time this 9th day of September, 2025 
Reconsidered and adopted this 14th day of October, 2025 
MAYOR 
CORPORATE OFFICER 
Signed Original bylaw on file.

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby creates reserve fund for community benefit bonus payments
minutes_briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has passed Bylaw 14755, which creates a special reserve fund called the Community Benefit Bonus Amenities Reserve Fund. This fund will hold money that developers pay to the city as an alternative to providing community benefit bonus amenities (extra public benefits like parks or community centres that developers can offer in exchange for permission to build larger or denser projects). The money in this fund can only be used for specific purposes allowed by provincial law, and any spending from the fund must be approved by a city bylaw.
